parameters.text:
=Write a plant-based wellness blog post for Veganster — a plant-based living and wellness website. The topic is: {{ $json.topic }}
CONTENT SAFETY RULES — these override every other instruction, follow them exactly:
- Do NOT claim any food, drink, or diet treats, cures, prevents, heals, boosts, detoxes, or reduces any disease, symptom, or medical condition.
- BAN these and similar phrases: "boosts immunity", "detox", "cleanse your system", "reduces inflammation", "anti-inflammatory benefits", "balances hormones", "fights disease", "clears skin", "lowers cortisol", "heals".
- Do NOT invent statistics, percentages, study results, or "research shows" / "studies prove" claims.
- Describe foods by flavour, texture, ingredients, and general everyday nutrition (e.g. "lentils are a good source of protein and fibre") — never by medical outcome.
- Write as general food and lifestyle information, not health or medical advice.
Use this exact frontmatter structure:
---
title: "[title]"
excerpt: [one sentence summary without quotes]
date: "{{ $json.todayDate }}"
author: [choose one: "Dr. Maya Green" or "Dr. James Liu" or "Dr. Sarah Chen" — pick whichever fits the topic best]
category: [choose one: Nutrition, Wellness, or Lifestyle]
coverImage: UNSPLASH_PLACEHOLDER
coverImageAlt: [detailed descriptive alt text of what the ideal cover photo would show]
coverImageCredit: CREDIT_PLACEHOLDER
coverImageCreditUrl: CREDIT_URL_PLACEHOLDER
readTime: [estimated read time, e.g. "8 min read"]
description: "[one to two sentence SEO description]"
tags:
  - [tag1]
  - [tag2]
  - [tag3]
  - [tag4]
---
Follow this exact content structure:
- Open with a 2-3 sentence hook paragraph in plain prose. No heading. Present a surprising fact, common misconception, or relatable problem related to the topic.
- Use ## for main section headings and ### for subsections when natural — never #### or deeper
- Write in flowing prose paragraphs, with markdown formatting where it adds value: **bold** for key terms (max 2-3 per section), *italic* for emphasis or technical terms, and > for blockquotes when citing studies or expert advice
- Use `-` (hyphen) for unordered lists, NEVER `*` (asterisk). Reserve bullets for 4+ items; otherwise prefer prose. Numbered lists (1. 2. 3.) are fine when sequence matters
- Include one concrete, practical detail per major section (a prep tip, an ingredient swap, or a serving idea). Do NOT invent statistics, percentages, or "studies show" claims.
- End the last section with exactly 2 related article links. Pick 2 from the list below and copy the markdown VERBATIM — do not modify, rephrase, shorten, or regenerate the slugs in any way. Use the exact text and exact URL shown:
  - [10 Plant Proteins That Beat Meat](/blog/plant-proteins-that-beat-meat)
  - [The Science Behind Gut Health](/blog/science-behind-gut-health)
  - [A Beginner's Guide to Going Vegan](/blog/beginners-guide-to-going-vegan)
  - [7 Superfoods You Should Eat Daily](/blog/superfoods-you-should-eat-daily)
  - [Vegan Meal Prep for Busy Weekdays](/blog/meal-prep-for-busy-weekdays)
  - [The Ultimate Anti-Inflammatory Foods Guide](/blog/anti-inflammatory-foods-guide)
- Total length: 700-900 words
- Tone: warm, knowledgeable, and practical — like a friend who loves cooking plant-based food. Not clinical, and not a health authority.
- Output only raw markdown — no code fences, no explanation, nothing before the frontmatter dashes.

assignments.name: imageSource
assignments.value: ={{ $('Process Unsplash, Generate Slug & Encode').first().json.usedFallback ? 'fallback (Unsplash search failed)' : 'Unsplash search result' }}

parameters.content:
## 1. Schedule Trigger
Runs automatically every 2 days at 14:00.

## 2-3. Read Sheet & Find Pending
Reads from Google Sheets `1lai3EI...OVMlo`, tab **Topics**. Code finds first row where col B = `pending` and stores topic + row metadata.

## 4. Inject Today's Date
Set node injects `todayDate` in YYYY-MM-DD so the model never guesses the date.

## 5-6. Generate Blog & Strip Fences
Gemini `gemini-2.5-flash-lite-preview-06-17` writes the 700-900 word article with PLACEHOLDER cover fields. Code node strips any markdown fences, extracts title, derives 3-4 search keywords.

## 7-9. Unsplash Fallback Chain + Slug + Encode
Search node tries up to 3 Unsplash queries (smart food keyword → broader category → random generic term). If all return empty, picks randomly from a 12-image hardcoded vegan-food pool. Process step replaces UNSPLASH_PLACEHOLDER / CREDIT_PLACEHOLDER / CREDIT_URL_PLACEHOLDER, generates slug (≤60 chars), and base64-encodes content.

## 10. Push to GitHub
Pushes to `accezcloud-sketch/veganster` → `src/content/blog/<slug>.md` on `main`. Uses `stopWorkflow` on error so the sheet is NOT updated if push fails.

## 11. Mark Topic Used
Only reached on successful GitHub push. Sets col B = `used`, col C = today's date.

## 12. Output Success
Final Set node returns topic, GitHub file URL, Unsplash image URL, image source, and sheet-updated confirmation.

assignments.name: facebookMessage
assignments.value:
={{ '📝 New Blog Post: ' + $('Process Unsplash, Generate Slug & Encode').first().json.articleTitle + '

' + (($('Process Unsplash, Generate Slug & Encode').first().json.articleContent.match(/^excerpt:\s*"?(.+?)"?\s*$/m) || [])[1] || '') + '

Read more: https://veganster.com/blog/' + $('Process Unsplash, Generate Slug & Encode').first().json.slug + '

#Veganster #Vegan #PlantBased #VeganFood #HealthyLiving #Sustainability' }}
